title: fix: harden app migration deploy workflow
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Uses the dedicated `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN_MYCELI_APPS` secret for app migration deploys instead of the shared Myceli token.
- Reads the real Cloudflare Pages project subdomain before creating Myceli origin CNAMEs.
- Makes Pages custom-domain provisioning and DNS upserts idempotent for reruns.
- Avoids invalid Pages project list pagination in the old account reclaim path.

## Validation
- `node --check apps/_scripts/deploy-app.js`
- `npx biome check --write apps/_scripts/deploy-app.js`
- `catgpt` workflow dispatch succeeded on this branch: https://github.com/pollinations/pollinations/actions/runs/26770353914
- Verified `catgpt.myceli.ai` and `catgpt.pollinations.ai` return 200

## Notes
- This is a follow-up to #11504 from the live canary rollout.
- No remaining app cutovers were run from this branch.

gist.headline: Hardening the hive's deploy pipelines
gist.blurb: We've fortified our app deployment scripts with dedicated tokens and idempotent DNS updates. Smoother automated rollouts for the community!
gist.summary: Updated the app deployment workflow to use a dedicated Cloudflare API token and made custom-domain provisioning idempotent for safer reruns.
gist.impact: This ensures more reliable and secure automated deployments for community apps without manual intervention.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot working on a sturdy, reinforced pipeline or server rack (representing deployment workflow) with the robot companion. Cloudflare/DNS motifs (clouds, gears).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny yellow hard hat, tightening a bolt on a glowing lime green (#ecf874) server rack. The chunky monitor-head robot stands nearby holding a clipboard with a checklist. Soft pastel gradient background with fluffy pixel clouds. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic, Stardew Valley vibes, emotionally warm.
